RegnalYear.Monarch: Elizabeth II 芒聙聯 1-2 Charles III

Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada National Parks Act, the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act, the Rouge National Urban Park Act and the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations
Identification.ShortTitle: Protecting Canada芒聙聶s Natural Wonders Act
Identification.RunningHead: Protecting Canada芒聙聶s Natural Wonders Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada National Parks Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: broaden and clarify offences in relation to the discharge or deposit of substances in a national park or national park reserve;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: expand the boundaries of seven national parks and one national park reserve;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: establish the Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada and enact provisions for its operation and administration; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: change the name of 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve of Canada芒聙聺 to 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve and Haida Heritage Site芒聙聺.

The enactment also amends the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act to establish the Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area and the Rouge National Urban Park Act to broaden and clarify offences in relation to the discharge or deposit of substances in that Park and makes consequential amendments to the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Canada National Parks Act, the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act, the Rouge National Urban Park Act and the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations

Text.XRefExternal: Protecting Canada芒聙聶s Natural Wonders Act
Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act

TableOfProvisions.Text: Consequential Amendments to the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations
Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada
Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Canada芒聙聶s Natural Wonders Act.
Paragraph 16(1)(t) of the Canada National Parks Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: the use, transportation and storage of pesticides and other toxic substances, including products treated with or containing any of those substances;

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Text: 脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: the use, transportation and temporary storage of pesticides and other toxic substances;

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 24(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes section 13, subsection 32(1) or (2) or a provision of the regulations designated by regulations made under paragraph 16(1)(y) or who fails to take measures that they are ordered to take under subsection 32(3) is guilty of an offence and liable
Every person who contravenes section 13, subsection 32(1) or a provision of the regulations designated by regulations made under paragraph 16(1)(y) is guilty of an offence and liable

Section.Text: Section 32 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited to discharge or deposit a substance in a park if the discharge or deposit degrades the natural environment, injures fauna, flora or cultural resources, endangers human health or public safety or is likely to do any of those things.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to notify and take measures
Subsection.Text: If the discharge or deposit of a substance in a park degrades the natural environment, injures fauna, flora or cultural resources, endangers human health or public safety or is likely to do any of those things, any person who has charge, management or control of the substance and any person who caused or contributed to the discharge or deposit shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notify the superintendent without delay; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take reasonable measures to prevent, mitigate or remediate harm and to prevent or minimize danger, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: If the superintendent of the park is of the opinion that a person is not taking the measures required by subsection (2)(b), the superintendent may order the person to take those measures. If the person fails to take the measures that they are ordered to take, the Minister may take the measures on behalf of His Majesty in right of Canada.

A person who fails to take the measures that they are ordered to take is liable for the expenses reasonably incurred by His Majesty in right of Canada in taking the measures. Those expenses may be recovered from the person, with costs, in proceedings brought in the name of His Majesty in any court of competent jurisdiction.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 4:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 32:

Subsection.Text: Where a substance that is capable of degrading the natural environment, injuring fauna, flora or cultural resources or endangering human health is discharged or deposited in a park, any person who has charge, management or control of the substance shall take reasonable measures to prevent any degradation of the natural environment and any danger to the fauna, flora or cultural resources or to persons that may result from the discharge or deposit.

If the superintendent of a park is of the opinion that a person is not taking the measures required by subsection (1), the superintendent may direct the person to take those measures and, if the person fails to do so, the Minister may direct those measures to be taken on behalf of Her Majesty in right of Canada.

A person who fails to comply with a direction given by a superintendent under subsection (2) is liable for the expenses reasonably incurred by Her Majesty in right of Canada in taking the measures directed, and those expenses may be recovered from that person, with costs, in proceedings brought in the name of Her Majesty in any court of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: Section 39 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to sections 40 to 41.7, this Act applies to a park reserve as if it were a park.
Subject to sections 40 to 41.5, this Act applies to a park reserve as if it were a park.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41.5:

Section.MarginalNote: Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections 5(1) and 6(2), leases, licences of occupation, easements, permits and other licences and authorizations relating to public lands in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada are deemed not to encumber or affect title to those lands, but if those lands become a park, those instruments continue in effect according to their terms and conditions.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Continuation of leases and licences of occupation
Subsection.Text: Existing leases and licences of occupation in or on Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada are continued under this Act in accordance with their terms and conditions, which prevail over this Act to the extent of any inconsistency between them.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Renewals 芒聙聰 leases and licences of occupation
Subsection.Text: Those leases and licences of occupation may be renewed in accordance with their terms and conditions. If a lease or licence of occupation does not provide for its renewal, then it may be renewed in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Leases or licences 芒聙聰 cabins
Subsection.Text: The Minister may enter into leases of public lands in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada for the personal use or occupation of cabins, or may issue licences for the personal use or occupation of cabins on those lands, and may renew or approve the assignment of those leases or licences.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Leases or licences 芒聙聰 tilts
Subsection.Text: The Minister may enter into leases of public lands in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada for the establishment or personal use or occupation of tilts, or may issue licences for the establishment, personal use or occupation of tilts on those lands, and may renew or approve the assignment of those leases or licences.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Authorizations 芒聙聰 cabins or tilts
Subsection.Text: The superintendent of Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada may authorize the personal use or occupation of cabins on public lands in the park reserve, or the establishment, personal use or occupation of tilts on those lands, and may renew or approve the assignment of those authorizations.

Section.MarginalNote: Activities 芒聙聰 Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve

Subsection.Text: A traditional land user who has in their possession a document issued by the superintendent that confirms that status may, subject to the following requirements, conditions and restrictions, carry on a traditional activity on public lands in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the requirements set out in or imposed under the provisions of this Act or the regulations, other than the provisions that do not apply in respect of that activity under subsection (3); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any conditions or restrictions imposed by the superintendent for the purposes of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: conservation, sustainable use or public health or safety, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: implementing any provision of a land claims agreement that requires, directly or indirectly, that commercial trapping by all, or by a class of, traditional land users be prohibited or restricted.

Subsection.Text: If, on application, the superintendent is satisfied that an individual is a traditional land user, the superintendent must issue a document confirming that status. The superintendent may subsequently revoke the document if the superintendent determines that the individual is no longer, or never was, a traditional land user.
The following provisions do not apply in respect of the following traditional activities that are carried on under subsection (1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 3(1)(a) of the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations in respect of fishing, including ice fishing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 41(1)(a) of the National Parks Highway Traffic Regulations in respect of the use of over-snow vehicles to access cabins or carry on other traditional activities;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 10 of the National Parks General Regulations in respect of berry picking, the gathering of medicinal plants and the cutting of wood for personal use;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 21 of the National Parks General Regulations in respect of motor boating;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 3 of the National Parks of Canada Camping Regulations in respect of camping;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 4(1) of the National Parks of Canada Fire Protection Regulations in respect of the use of fires to heat food or beverages;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 4(1)(a) and (c) and section 19 of the National Parks Wildlife Regulations in respect of the hunting of ducks, geese, ptarmigan, grouse and porcupine, trapping, including for commercial purposes, and the snaring of snowshoe hare and ptarmigan;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 20(1), (5) and (6) and paragraph 21(b) of the National Parks Wildlife Regulations in respect of the hunting of ducks, geese, ptarmigan, grouse and porcupine, trapping, including for commercial purposes, and the transport and use of firearms for personal safety, hunting and trapping; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 20(5) and paragraph 21(b) of the National Parks Wildlife Regulations in respect of the snaring of snowshoe hare and ptarmigan.

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act or the regulations, Inuit referred to in section 12.13.10 of the Agreement may carry on the harvesting activities permitted by that section, in the portion of Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada that overlaps the area set out in the Map Atlas referred to in that section, if those activities are carried on in accordance with the Agreement and the laws of Newfoundland and Labrador, including the orders and regulations referred to in paragraph 1 of schedule 12-F to the Agreement.

The laws of Newfoundland and Labrador apply to Inuit referred to in subsection (4), with respect to the harvesting activities referred to in that subsection that are carried on in the portion of Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada referred to in that subsection, if the effect of those laws is to implement schedule 12-F to the Agreement.
Subsection (5) does not apply in respect of Inuit referred to in subsection (4) who are carrying on traditional activities under subsection (1).

Despite any other provision of this Act or the regulations, a person may conduct a take-off or landing of an aircraft, as defined in subsection 3(1) of the Aeronautics Act, on public lands in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada if they conduct it in accordance with an authorization provided by the superintendent.

The superintendent must have regard to considerations of public health and safety and the sustainable use and conservation of Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada when authorizing a take-off or landing.
In the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any reference to Gros Morne National Park of Canada, except in Schedules IV and V to those Regulations, is deemed to include a reference to Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the text set out in item 48, column 1, of Schedule II to those Regulations is deemed to include a reference to the waters referred to in Schedule I to the Newfoundland and Labrador Fishery Regulations that are wholly or partially located in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada and the open season for those waters is, despite the text set out in column III, deemed to be June 15 to September 15;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the open season for all other waters that are located in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada is, despite the text set out in item 49, column III, of Schedule II to those Regulations, deemed to be February 1 to September 15; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: items 1 and 2, column II, of Schedule V to those Regulations are deemed to include a reference to the waters referred to in Schedule I to the Newfoundland and Labrador Fishery Regulations that are wholly or partially located in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: Agreement芒聙聜means the land claims agreement signed on behalf of Inuit of Labrador, Her Majesty the Queen in right of Newfoundland and Labrador and Her Majesty the Queen in right of Canada on January 22, 2005, including any amendments made to it.芒聙聜(Accord)
common-law partner, in relation to an individual, means another individual who has been cohabiting with the individual in a conjugal relationship for at least one year.芒聙聜(conjoint de fait)

designated area芒聙聜means the public lands located in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada, all lands within a 50 km wide perimeter surrounding the park reserve and the community of Happy Valley-Goose Bay.芒聙聜(zone d脙漏sign脙漏e)
superintendent芒聙聜means the superintendent of Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada.芒聙聜(directeur)
traditional activity芒聙聜means any of the following activities:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the use of an over-snow vehicle, as defined in section 2 of the National Parks Highway Traffic Regulations, to access cabins or carry on other traditional activities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fishing, including ice fishing;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: berry picking;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: motor boating;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the gathering of medicinal plants;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the use of fires to heat food or beverages;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the hunting of ducks, geese, ptarmigan, grouse and porcupine;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: trapping, including for commercial purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the snaring of snowshoe hare and ptarmigan;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the transport and use of firearms and ammunition for personal safety, hunting and trapping;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the cutting of wood for personal use; and

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: camping.芒聙聜(activit脙漏 traditionnelle)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a beneficiary, as defined in section 1.1.1 of the Agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who is a member of NunatuKavut Community Council Inc., a corporation without share capital incorporated under the laws of Newfoundland and Labrador;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who was born in the designated area;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who was born to a biological parent who, at the time of the individual芒聙聶s birth, was ordinarily resident in the designated area;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who was legally adopted by a parent who, at the time the adoption took effect, was ordinarily resident in the designated area;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who was or has been ordinarily resident in the designated area for at least 10 consecutive years; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a child, spouse or common-law partner of an individual described in any of paragraphs (a) to (f).芒聙聜(utilisateur traditionnel des terres)

Section.Text: Sections 41.6 and 41.7 continue to apply, with any adaptations that may be necessary, to any public lands in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada that become a park.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 41.7(7) and (8) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection 41.7(9) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the description of Riding Mountain National Park of Canada in Part 4 of Schedule 1 to the Act that begins with 芒聙聹(b) sections 25, 26, 27芒聙聺 and ends with 芒聙聹subdivisions 13, 14, 15 and 16 of Section 27;芒聙聺 is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The first, second and third paragraphs of the description of Thousand Islands National Park of Canada in Part 5 of Schedule 1 to the Act are replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: In the Province of Ontario, Municipalities of the Township of Leeds and the Thousand Islands, and the Township of Front of Yonge, and the City of Brockville, County of Leeds and Grenville, more particularly described as follows:
All those parcels of land being more particularly described under Firstly to Twenty-ninthly as follows:

Subsection.Text: The portion of the description of Thousand Islands National Park of Canada in Part 5 of Schedule 1 to the French version of the Act that begins with 芒聙聹Neuvi脙篓mement芒聙聺 and ends with 芒聙聹ladite partie refermant environ 0,40 hectare芒聙聺 is amended by replacing 芒聙聹concession de Broken Front芒聙聺 with 芒聙聹interrompue en front芒聙聺.
The last paragraph of the description of Thousand Islands National Park of Canada in Part 5 of Schedule 1 to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Twenty-thirdly

Section.Text: The description of Mingan Archipelago National Park Reserve of Canada in Schedule 2 to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: In the Province of Quebec:

All those lands being the whole and entire Fief and Seigneurie of the Isles and Islets of Mingan, situate and being on the coast of the North shore of the St. Lawrence River, in the Province of Quebec, the said Fief and Seigneurie consisting of the Isles and Islets which being on the said North shore of the St. Lawrence River from Perroquets Island, longitude 64脗掳12芒聙虏40芒聙鲁 following continuation to the mouth of the River Aguanish, longitude 62脗掳05芒聙虏00芒聙鲁 in such manner as the said Fief and Seigneurie with its rights is more amply conceded and designated by the title of concession granted to Jacques de Lalande and Louis Joliet.

Some of the Isles and Islets forming part of the Fief and Seigneurie of the Isles and Islets of Mingan are now known and designated as being lots 5 338 113 to 5 338 121, 6 504 068 and 6 504 185 to 6 504 187 of the cadastre of Quebec. The other Isles and Islets, forming part of the Fief and Seigneurie of the Isles and Islets of Mingan, are without cadastral designation.

Notwithstanding the generality of the foregoing, the following Isles and Islets forming part of the Fief and Seigneurie of the Isles and Islets of Mingan are not included in the present description, namely: Maison Island, Wreck Island (without cadastral designation), Havre de Mingan Island designated as being lot 6 504 191 of the cadastre of Quebec and part of Firmin Island designated as being lot 5 338 112 of the cadastre of Quebec.
Those lands included in the present description containing about 97.7 square kilometres exclude the lands situated below the ordinary high-water mark of the said Isles and Islets.
The longitudes mentioned above were scaled from the 1:250,000 map sheets 12L and 22I of the national topographic series.

Section.Text: Schedule 2 to the Act is amended by adding the following after the description of Sable Island National Park Reserve of Canada:

Provision.Text: All that lot, piece or parcel of land situated in the electoral districts of Cartwright-L芒聙聶Anse au Clair, Torngat Mountains and Lake Melville, in the Province of Newfoundland and Labrador, described in Chapter 1 of the Land Description Report for the Proposed National Park Reserve in the Mealy Mountains Region of Labrador recorded in the Canada Lands Survey Records as FB40974 CLSR on January 7, 2016, and shown on the plan prepared by the Atlantic Regional Office of the Surveyor General Branch, Natural Resources Canada, dated between December 22 and 24, 2015, entitled 芒聙聹Descriptive Map Plan showing the Boundary of the Proposed National Park Reserve in the Mealy Mountains Region of Labrador芒聙聺. A copy of the said plan was recorded in the Registry of Crown Titles in St. John芒聙聶s, Newfoundland and Labrador, as Special Plan SP 478R. A copy of the said plan was also recorded in the Canada Lands Surveys Records in Ottawa, Ontario, under number 104838 CLSR on January 7, 2016.
The said National Park Reserve contains an area of approximately 10 700 square kilometres.

Section.Text: Schedule 5 to the Act is amended by deleting the description of the Mount Agassiz Ski Area and the heading before it.
The Act is amended by replacing 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve of Canada芒聙聺 with 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve and Haida Heritage Site芒聙聺 in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 41; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the heading 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve of Canada芒聙聺 in Schedule 2.

Heading.TitleText: Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act

Section.Text: Schedule 1 to the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act is amended by adding, after the heading of that Schedule, the text set out in the schedule to this Act.
Section 17 of the Rouge National Urban Park Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited to discharge or deposit a substance in the Park if the discharge or deposit degrades the natural environment, injures natural or cultural resources, endangers human health or public safety or is likely to do any of those things.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to notify and take reasonable measures
Subsection.Text: If the discharge or deposit of a substance in the Park degrades the natural environment, injures natural or cultural resources, endangers human health or public safety or is likely to do any of those things, any person who has charge, management or control of the substance and any person who caused or contributed to the discharge or deposit must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notify the superintendent without delay; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take reasonable measures to prevent, mitigate or remediate harm and to prevent or minimize danger, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: If the superintendent is of the opinion that a person is not taking the required measures, the superintendent may order the person to take those measures. If the person fails to take the measures that they are ordered to take, the Minister may take the measures on behalf of His Majesty in right of Canada.

A person who fails to take the measures that they are ordered to take is liable for the expenses reasonably incurred by His Majesty in right of Canada in taking the measures. Those expenses may be recovered from the person, with costs, in proceedings brought in the name of His Majesty in any court of competent jurisdiction.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 19:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 17:

Subsection.Text: If a substance that is capable of degrading the natural environment, injuring natural or cultural resources or endangering human health is discharged or deposited in the Park, any person who has charge, management or control of the substance and any person who caused or contributed to the discharge or deposit must take reasonable measures to prevent any degradation of the natural environment and any danger to natural or cultural resources or to human health that may result from the discharge or deposit.

If the superintendent is of the opinion that a person is not taking the required measures, the superintendent may order the person to take those measures and, if the person fails to do so, the Minister may take those measures on behalf of Her Majesty in right of Canada.

A person who fails to comply with an order given by the superintendent is liable for the expenses reasonably incurred by Her Majesty in right of Canada in taking the measures, and those expenses may be recovered from that person, with costs, in proceedings brought in the name of Her Majesty in any court of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: Section 19 of the Act is replaced by the following:
For greater certainty, subsections 17(1) and (2) and 18(2) do not prevent the carrying out of agricultural activities by a lessee of public lands in the Park in accordance with their lease.
For greater certainty, subsections 17(1) and 18(2) do not prevent the carrying out of agricultural activities by a lessee of public lands in the Park in accordance with their lease.
The portion of subsection 33(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection 17(1) or (2) or who fails to take measures that they are ordered to take under subsection 17(3) is guilty of an offence and liable
Every person who contravenes subsection 17(1) is guilty of an offence and liable

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendments to the National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations

Section.Text: The National Parks of Canada Fishing Regulations are amended by replacing 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve of Canada芒聙聺 with 芒聙聹Gwaii Haanas National Park Reserve and Haida Heritage Site芒聙聺 in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 2.6; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: item 15 of Schedule IV.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Lake Superior National Marine Conservation Area Act.
If section 4 of the other Act comes into force before section 18 of this Act, that section 18 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Schedule 1 to the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act is amended by adding, after the description of Lake Superior National Marine Conservation Area of Canada, the text set out in the schedule to this Act.

Subsection.Text: If section 18 of this Act comes into force before section 4 of the other Act, the portion of that section 4 preceding the title 芒聙聹Lake Superior National Marine Conservation Area of Canada芒聙聺 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Schedule 1 to the Act is amended by adding the following after the heading of that Schedule:

Subsection.Text: If section 4 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 18 of this Act, then that section 4 is deemed to have come into force before that section 18 and subsection (2) applies as a consequence.

Section.MarginalNote: Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(2) comes into force on the day on which regulations made under the Canada National Parks Act respecting access by air to Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada, or to any future national park that replaces all or part of that park reserve, come into force.

Subsection 6(3) comes into force on the day on which regulations made under the Canada National Parks Act respecting recreational fishing in Akami-Uapishk谩碌聛芒聙聯KakKasuak芒聙聯Mealy Mountains National Park Reserve of Canada, or in any future national park that replaces all or part of that park reserve, come into force.

Section.MarginalNote: Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area
Section.Text: Section 18 comes into force on the day on which a notice is published in the Canada Gazette confirming that an interim management plan for the Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area has been approved by the Aulattiqatigiit Board, as defined in section 2.2 of the Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area Inuit Impact and Benefit Agreement signed on behalf of Inuit of the Qikiqtani Region of Nunavut and Her Majesty the Queen in Right of Canada on August 1, 2019.

GroupHeading.TitleText: Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area

Containing an area of approximately 108 000 square kilometres as measured on the Albers Equal Area Projection;
Save and except all islands, rocks and shoals that are 400 hectares in area or larger as measured from the ordinary high water mark;
Save and except the Nirjutiqarvik National Wildlife Area as defined in the Wildlife Area Regulations pursuant to the Canada Wildlife Act;
Save and except the Prince Leopold Island Bird Sanctuary as defined in the Migratory Bird Sanctuary Regulations pursuant to the Migratory Birds Convention Act, 1994;
Save and except all Inuit Owned Lands that are wholly or partially within the hereinbefore described Tallurutiup Imanga National Marine Conservation Area;
References to straight lines mean points joined directly on the NAD83 ellipsoid by geodesics;
Coordinates referenced hereon are geodetic and referenced to the North American Datum of 1983 (NAD83), Canadian Spatial Reference System (CSRS).
